---
title: "Evidently — A Rundown for AiSmartOps"
---

## 1. What Evidently is, in one paragraph

Evidently is an open-source Python framework for evaluating, testing, and monitoring ML/LLM systems (Apache 2.0, ~6.4k+ stars, very actively maintained — current version is 0.7.x). It ships 100+ built-in metrics for data drift, data quality, and model performance, plus a self-hostable monitoring UI. It is deliberately modular: you can use it as a one-off Python library, as a CI/CD gate, or as a full monitoring service — without committing to any of those tiers up front.

## 2. The three-layer mental model

This is the most important thing to internalize. Evidently has one input pattern (a Dataset) and three escalating output modes:

```text
 ┌──────────────────────────────────┐
 │ INPUT: Pandas DataFrame(s)       │
 │ ─ current_data (required)        │
 │ ─ reference_data (optional,      │
 │   usually training/baseline)     │
 └─────────────┬────────────────────┘
               │
 ┌─────────────┼──────────────────────────┐
 ▼             ▼                          ▼
 ┌───────────┐ ┌───────────┐ ┌──────────────┐
 │ REPORT    │ │ TEST SUITE│ │ MONITORING   │
 │ raw       │ │ pass/fail │ │ DASHBOARD    │
 │ metrics   │ │ over time │ │ time-series  │
 │ (HTML/    │ │ Report    │ │ panels +     │
 │ JSON)     │ │           │ │ alerts       │
 └───────────┘ └───────────┘ └──────────────┘
 "describe"   "validate"    "surveil"

 Best for:    Best for:       Best for:
 ─ EDA        ─ CI/CD gates   ─ Production PMS
 ─ Debugging  ─ Pre-deploy    ─ Trend tracking
 ─ Model      checks          ─ Alerting
   cards      ─ Regression
                tests
```

A Report is a snapshot (“here are 30 metrics”). Add pass/fail thresholds and the same Report becomes a Test Suite (“did each metric stay inside its bound?”). Snapshots persisted as JSON over time become a Monitoring Dashboard. One mental model, three deployment intensities.

## 3. The metric catalog (what you actually get)

```text
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DATA DRIFT             │ Per-column statistical tests              │
│                        │ Numerical: KS, Wasserstein, PSI           │
│                        │ Categorical: Chi², Jensen-Shannon, PSI    │
│                        │ Text/embed: embedding drift, MMD-lite     │
├────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ DATA QUALITY           │ Missing %, duplicates, range violations,  │
│                        │ new categories, correlations, cardinality │
├────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ MODEL PERFORMANCE      │ Classification: AUC, F1, precision/recall │
│                        │ confusion matrix, calibration curves      │
│                        │ Regression: MAE, MAPE, error bias         │
│                        │ Ranking/RecSys: NDCG, MAP, hit rate       │
├────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ TARGET / PREDICTION    │ Drift in y_true and y_pred distributions  │
│ DRIFT                  │ (works even before ground truth arrives)  │
├────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ TEXT / LLM             │ Length, sentiment, toxicity, regex match, │
│                        │ semantic similarity, LLM-as-judge         │
└────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## 4. Why the OSS report calls it your “monitoring backbone”

Looking at the [OSS landscape chapter](../landscapes/OSS_Landscape_BuildingBlock_DeepResearch.qmd), Evidently is the only single tool that scores well on all four of these criteria simultaneously: (a) Apache 2.0 (no AGPL/BSL/SSPL footguns), (b) covers both PIE and PMS, (c) self-hostable on hospital infrastructure, and (d) modular enough that you adopt incrementally. Deepchecks is AGPL, Alibi Detect went BSL, NannyML covers a narrower slice, whylogs is profiling-only. Evidently sits at the intersection.

## 5. How Evidently maps to your PIE → PMS lifecycle

This is the part that matters most for AiSmartOps. The Evidently Report → TestSuite → Dashboard pipeline is isomorphic to your PIE → Go/No-Go → PMS workflow:

```text
 PIE PHASE                 GO/NO-GO              PMS PHASE
 (Pre-Implementation       DECISION GATE         (Post-Market
 Evaluation)                                     Surveillance)
 ──────────────────        ──────────────        ──────────────

 ┌───────────────────┐     ┌───────────────┐     ┌────────────────┐
 │ Vendor's hospital │     │ All test      │     │ Live inference │
 │ silent-run output │     │ conditions    │ YES │ logs (daily    │
 │ on local data     │     │ pass on local │ ───►│ batches)       │
 │ + reference set   │     │ validation    │     │                │
 │                   │     │ dataset?      │     │                │
 └─────────┬─────────┘     └───────────────┘     └─────────┬──────┘
           │                       ▲                       │
           ▼                       │                       ▼
 ┌───────────────────┐     ┌───────────────┐     ┌────────────────┐
 │ Evidently         │     │ Evidently     │     │ Evidently      │
 │ Test Suite        │────►│ pass/fail     │     │ snapshots →    │
 │                   │     │ summary       │     │ Monitoring UI  │
 │ ─ Data quality    │     │ (audit-       │     │                │
 │ ─ Data drift vs   │     │ loggable      │     │ ─ Daily drift  │
 │   training set    │     │ artifact)     │     │ ─ Perf trend   │
 │ ─ Performance vs  │     └───────────────┘     │ ─ Alert rules  │
 │   threshold       │                           │ ─ Bias audit   │
 │ ─ Subgroup parity │                           └────────┬───────┘
 └───────────────────┘                                    │
                                                          ▼
                                                ┌────────────────┐
                                                │ Alert → Re-PIE │
                                                │ on drift > X   │
                                                └────────────────┘
```

The key architectural payoff: the same metric definitions flow through both phases. A threshold you proved acceptable in PIE becomes the alert boundary in PMS automatically — no rewriting, no two parallel evaluation codebases. This is exactly the auditability story your funding proposal needs for ISO 13485 / IEC 62304 / ISO/IEC 42001 compliance.

## 6. Where Evidently slots into the AiSmartOps stack

```text
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  AI SMART ORCHESTRATION PLATFORM                    │
│                                                                     │
│ ┌────────────────┐ ┌────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────┐           │
│ │ INGESTION      │►│ ORCHESTRATION  │►│ INFERENCE       │           │
│ │ MONAI Inform.  │ │ MONAI Workflow │ │ Triton/KServe   │           │
│ │ Gateway, FHIR  │ │ Manager        │ │                 │           │
│ └────────────────┘ └────────┬───────┘ └────────┬────────┘           │
│                             │                  │                    │
│                             ▼                  ▼                    │
│              ┌──────────────────────────────────┐                   │
│              │ FEATURE EXTRACTION ADAPTER       │                   │
│              │ (lifts model output to a         │                   │
│              │ tabular schema Evidently         │                   │
│              │ can read — see §7)               │                   │
│              └────────────────┬─────────────────┘                   │
│                               │                                     │
│                               ▼                                     │
│     ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐             │
│     │                 ◄◄◄ EVIDENTLY ►►►               │             │
│     │                                                 │             │
│     │ PIE Test Suite (pre-deploy gate)                │             │
│     │ PMS Monitoring Dashboard (live)                 │             │
│     │                                                 │             │
│     │ Self-hosted on hospital VM/K8s,                 │             │
│     │ data never leaves the firewall.                 │             │
│     └────┬────────────────────────────────────────┬───┘             │
│          │                                        │                 │
│          ▼                                        ▼                 │
│ ┌────────────────┐                      ┌────────────────┐          │
│ │ MLflow         │                      │ Grafana /      │          │
│ │ (registry +    │                      │ OHIF Viewer    │          │
│ │ lineage)       │                      │ (clinician)    │          │
│ └────────────────┘                      └────────────────┘          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## 7. The radiology-specific caveat (this is the important one)

Evidently is tabular-first by design. It will not compute pixel-level drift on a CT volume the way Alibi Detect’s MMD-on-deep-features will. For a radiology orchestration platform, this means you cannot just hand Evidently a folder of DICOMs.

What you do instead — and what makes Evidently still the right backbone — is build a feature-extraction adapter that lifts each inference event into a flat row:

```text
Per-inference row (one row per study):
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Metadata: modality, body_part, manufacturer, kvp,                    │
│ slice_thickness, station_name, scanner_model                         │
│ Demographics: age_bin, sex, weight_bin (if available)                │
│ Image stats: mean_intensity, std, mean_HU_lung, snr_estimate         │
│ Model output: predicted_class, top_k_probabilities, max_logit,       │
│ bbox_count, segmentation_volume_ml                                   │
│ Operational: latency_ms, gpu_util, model_version                     │
│ Ground truth: radiologist_label (delayed; arrives later)             │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Evidently then monitors drift on this derived schema, which is exactly what FDA-style PMS asks for: “is the input distribution the model is seeing today the same as the distribution it was validated on?” You watch scanner-model drift, intensity-stat drift, prediction-confidence drift, latency drift — all at the orchestrator level, model-agnostic.

This dovetails directly with the [Published Languages chapter](../landscapes/RadAI_Published_Languages_Report.qmd) insight: because there is no Published Language for radiology AI inference outputs, your platform must define one locally. The Evidently-row schema above effectively becomes that internal published language — a stable tabular contract that every radiology model adapter must produce, regardless of whether the upstream model is a YOLO box-tensor, a SAM mask, or a SR-style classifier. This is a meaningful architectural win, not just plumbing.

## 8. What Evidently does not solve (so you know the gaps)

```text
NOT covered by Evidently                  → Pair with
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Pixel-level image drift                   Alibi Detect (BSL — eval license)
Perf estimation w/o ground truth          NannyML (the canonical pairing)
Privacy-preserving profiling              whylogs (no raw data leaves site)
Comprehensive fairness suite              AIF360 + Fairlearn
Pre-deploy adversarial scan               Giskard
DICOM-native rendering                    OHIF / Cornerstone3D
```

The OSS report’s headline pairing — Evidently + NannyML — exists for one specific reason: in radiology, ground truth arrives weeks-to-months late (follow-up imaging, biopsy, clinical outcome). Evidently tells you the inputs drifted; NannyML estimates whether that drift hurt your AUC before the labels return. Together they cover the full FUTURE-AI Robustness principle.

## 9. Minimal hello-world (so the API isn’t abstract)

```python
import pandas as pd
from evidently import Report
from evidently.presets import DataDriftPreset, ClassificationPreset

# reference = silent-run / training distribution
# current = last 7 days of production inferences
report = Report(metrics=[
    DataDriftPreset(),
    ClassificationPreset(),
])

snapshot = report.run(reference_data=ref_df, current_data=prod_df)
snapshot.save_html("pms_week_2026_w17.html")  # for radiologists
snapshot.save_json("pms_week_2026_w17.json")  # for the dashboard
```

To turn this into a PIE gate, you add Tests (e.g. `gt`, `lt`, `eq`) and the same object emits a pass/fail summary CI can block on. Same code, two roles.

Bottom line for AiSmartOps: adopt Evidently as the evaluation kernel of OBJ-2 (PIE + PMS), wrap it with a thin radiology feature-extraction adapter that becomes your platform’s internal published language, and reserve Alibi Detect / NannyML / whylogs for the gaps Evidently leaves open. The Apache 2.0 license, self-hostability, and identical metric definitions across PIE and PMS are what make it a defensible, audit-friendly choice — not just a popular one.
